text.title: task-worktree-runtime-isolation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for task-worktree-runtime-isolation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Reusing the same worktree across sequential tasks "to save the venv install" without resetting branch / scope.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Branch state from task N leaks into task N+1; the next merge contains accidental commits from the prior task and the scope file is stale.

failure-mode.description: Allocating ports randomly per run instead of deterministically per slug, so a re-attached worktree gets a different port and the running dev server is orphaned.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Determinism per slug is what makes the worktree resumable across crashes and restarts; randomness leaks dangling processes that bind ports the next agent expects.
